transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Good morning, what brings you here in today?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Good morning, Doctor. I have been having a headache and pain in my eyes.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Since when you have been experiencing this?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It started about two days ago.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Is the headache constant or does it come and go?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It's mostly constant but it gets worse when I look at the screens.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Where exactly is the headache located?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Mostly around my forehead and behind my eyes.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: Do you have any other symptoms like fever, nausea or blood, vision?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No fever, but sometimes my vision feels slightly strained.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: How many hours do you spend on screens daily?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Around eight to ten hours I work on a laptop.

transcript.speaker: Doctor
transcript.text: That could be causing eye strain.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Not really, maybe five to six hours.

report_data.symptoms: headache, pain in eyes, strained vision, eye strain
report_data.clinical_observations: Patient reports constant headache, worse with screen time, eye strain, and strained vision. No fever or other symptoms.
report_data.diagnosis: Possible eye strain, differential diagnoses: migraine, sinusitis
report_data.treatment_advice: Reduce screen time, get enough sleep (7-8 hours), consider eye drops for relief
report_data.action_plan: Follow-up in 2 days, monitor symptoms, consider eye examination if symptoms persist
report_data.verification_notes: Patient confirmed symptoms, denied fever or other symptoms
report_data.doctor_notes: Patient's symptoms are likely related to prolonged screen time and inadequate sleep. Consider eye examination if symptoms persist.
